ops.title: A subagent's session-limit 'failed' notice is not permanent death — a reset revives it; hard-stop before re-dispatching a replacement or they collide
ops.body:
A background subagent that emits an `idle_notification` with `idleReason: "failed"` / `"session limit"` is **paused, not dead**. When the limit resets, the runtime can **revive it mid-task**, and it resumes from where it left off (including executing an instruction you sent just before it paused).

**Why:** During the experiment-at-home D3a (stream-trade U-MODAL) work (2026-07-14), an agent `d3a-modal` hit its session limit right after I told it "take option A." I read the failure notice as terminal and spawned a fresh replacement (`d3a-modal-2`) onto the *same* task in the *same* working tree. The limit then reset, `d3a-modal` revived and resumed executing "take A," and the two agents clobbered each other's edits — `modal_app.py` rewritten under the replacement, its `Write`s failing "file modified since read," the working-copy commit advancing on its own. I had even told the replacement "the other agent is dead, you're the sole worker"; it correctly **reproduced the collision with hard evidence and pushed back** instead of deferring to my wrong call (the mirror of [[dont-retract-a-reproduced-finding-under-pushback]] — here the subordinate was right and the authority wrong).

**How to apply:** Before re-dispatching a replacement for an agent that "failed" on a limit, do one of: (a) **hard-stop the original first** (`TaskStop <name>`) so a reset can't revive it into a collision, or (b) **wait for the reset and check whether the original resumed** (its files/commits advancing is the tell) before spawning anything. Never run two agents on the same working tree — shared tree *and* shared `.venv` means even one agent's `uv sync` (e.g. dropping an extra like `live`) corrupts the other's baseline. When a peer reports a collision with concrete evidence (files it never touched changing, write-conflict errors), believe the reproduction immediately and stop one worker — don't restate your prior "it's dead" claim. Related: [[dont-retract-a-reproduced-finding-under-pushback]].
